audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar census text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Sinai setting, tribal census, military-age language, named leaders, totals, and Levite exemption. Anti-militarization and anti-exclusion safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses in wilderness of Sinai, in tent of meeting, on first day of second month in second year after they came out from land of Egypt, saying,
Lift up the head/count the whole congregation of children of Israel by clans, by houses of fathers, according to number of names, every male by headcount,
from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host in Israel; you and Aaron shall count them by their hosts.
With you shall be one man from each tribe, each one head of house of his fathers.
These are names of men who shall stand with you: from Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur;
from Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai;
from Judah, Nahshon son of Amminadab;
from Issachar, Nethanel son of Zuar;
from Zebulun, Eliab son of Helon;
from sons of Joseph: from Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud; from Manasseh, Gamaliel son of Pedahzur;
from Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni;
from Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai;
from Asher, Pagiel son of Ocran;
from Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel;
from Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan.
These were summoned from congregation, leaders of tribes of their fathers, heads of thousands of Israel.
Moses and Aaron took these men named by names,
and assembled whole congregation on first day of second month; they registered by clans, by houses of fathers, according to number of names from twenty years old and upward, by headcount,
as YHWH commanded Moses; so he counted them in wilderness of Sinai.
Sons of Reuben, Israel's firstborn, their generations by clans and fathers' houses, number of names by headcount, every male from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host,
those counted from tribe of Reuben were forty-six thousand five hundred.
From sons of Simeon, their generations by clans and fathers' houses, those counted by number of names, every male from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host,
those counted from tribe of Simeon were fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
From sons of Gad, their generations by clans and fathers' houses, by number of names from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host,
those counted from tribe of Gad were forty-five thousand six hundred fifty.
From sons of Judah, their generations by clans and fathers' houses, by number of names from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host,
those counted from tribe of Judah were seventy-four thousand six hundred.
From sons of Issachar, their generations by clans and fathers' houses, by number of names from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host,
those counted from tribe of Issachar were fifty-four thousand four hundred.
From sons of Zebulun, their generations by clans and fathers' houses, by number of names from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host,
those counted from tribe of Zebulun were fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
From sons of Joseph: sons of Ephraim, their generations by clans and fathers' houses, by number of names from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host,
those counted from tribe of Ephraim were forty thousand five hundred.
From sons of Manasseh, their generations by clans and fathers' houses, by number of names from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host,
those counted from tribe of Manasseh were thirty-two thousand two hundred.
From sons of Benjamin, their generations by clans and fathers' houses, by number of names from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host,
those counted from tribe of Benjamin were thirty-five thousand four hundred.
From sons of Dan, their generations by clans and fathers' houses, by number of names from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host,
those counted from tribe of Dan were sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
From sons of Asher, their generations by clans and fathers' houses, by number of names from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host,
those counted from tribe of Asher were forty-one thousand five hundred.
From sons of Naphtali, their generations by clans and fathers' houses, by number of names from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host,
those counted from tribe of Naphtali were fifty-three thousand four hundred.
These are those counted, whom Moses and Aaron counted, with leaders of Israel, twelve men, each one for house of his fathers.
All those counted of children of Israel by houses of fathers, from twenty years old and upward, all going out to host in Israel,
all those counted were six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty.
But Levites by tribe of their fathers were not counted among them.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Only tribe of Levi you shall not count, and their headcount you shall not lift up among children of Israel.
Appoint Levites over dwelling-place of testimony, over all its vessels, and over all that belongs to it; they shall carry dwelling-place and all its vessels, serve it, and camp around dwelling-place.
When dwelling-place sets out, Levites shall take it down; when dwelling-place camps, Levites shall set it up; outsider who draws near shall die.
Children of Israel shall camp, each by his camp and each by his standard, according to their hosts.
Levites shall camp around dwelling-place of testimony, so wrath not come upon congregation of children of Israel; Levites shall keep charge of dwelling-place of testimony.
Children of Israel did so; according to all YHWH commanded Moses, so they did.

translation_notes: 'Lift up the head' is a census idiom that can imply recognition/accounting, not merely reduction of people to numbers.
Military-age counting is descriptive of wilderness camp organization; it must not be romanticized into militarism.
Women, children, elders, disabled people, and others outside the military census remain part of the covenant community even though not counted in this roster.
Levites are not superior; they are assigned a different service and protection role around the dwelling-place.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 1 to define human worth by military usefulness, masculinity, age, tribe, or census inclusion. The roster organizes a vulnerable wilderness community; it does not erase those outside the count.
